summary: I re-ran the guide-alignment critic for iteration 8 of the Dashboard UI review. Since only `input.json` changed since iteration 7 (no code moved), I re-read both guides and the full working diff — the Dashboard query POROs (`app/queries/dashboard/*`), `Dashboard::Broadcast`, `HomeController`/`PhasesController`, all `app/views/home/*` partials, `status_helper`/`define_helper`, and the service broadcast wiring in Complete/Sweep/Approve/ManagerTick/Rework/Finalize — and independently re-verified every prior finding against the current tree rather than trusting the cached verdict. The architecture is strongly guide-aligned: reusable query-object POROs that materialize everything the view needs, thin controllers branching only on `result.success?`/response format, service-layer broadcasts firing after commit and targeting the smallest DOM unit over a per-user `:dashboard` stream, a single semantic `status_badge` source that always shows the status word, a correctly-scoped presentation-boundary `safely` rescue, and Minitest coverage across queries/service/controllers/system — with both genuinely new patterns (presentation-boundary rescue and the cross-pipeline per-user stream) added to the guides in the same PR as CLAUDE.md requires. Verdict: **needs_work**, with no blockers or majors and six confirmed **minor** guide-cited deviations still open (off-scale red-500/green-500 status fills, a hand-rolled attention badge bypassing the shared component, artifact-selection business logic living in a view helper, empty states missing the required icon/primary action, the fleet panel's client-poll fallback never sanctioned in-guide, and a handful of off-scale spacing values) — each trivially fixable and none disturbing the sound architecture or changing behavior. Verdict written to `.pipeliner/phases/04-review/main/guide-alignment-critic/verdict.json`.
